The Town Manager performs assigned duties directly or through the supervision and direction of department heads and other trained staff. The Town Manager is responsible for recommending to the Council and Board of Finance an annual budget, and for the subsequent administration of the adopted budget. The Town Manager is also responsible to the Council for implementation of their policies, procedures, programs, and other directives. The Town Manager also provides the Council, Board of Finance, and other agencies, as necessary, with reports, recommendations, and guidance on municipal administration. The Town Manager maintains an immediate office staff of an Executive Assistant and a Human Resources Coordinator. The Town Manager can be reached at the Town Hall at 860 - 267 - 4468. Appointments with the Town Manager may be made with the Executive Assistant at the telephone number listed above, or by email . hours: Environmental Affairs Planning & Zoning Zoning Enforcement Related Departments How Do I… Home Government Departments A - L Land Use Planning & Zoning Planning & Zoning Department Outdoor Dining (Sidewalk Only) Season Now Open - Approvals Required The Town’s Outdoor Dining (Sidewalk Only) season is now officially underway and runs from May 22 through October 4 this year. All businesses wishing to offer outdoor seating must obtain approvals before any outdoor setup begins. Key requirements include: Eligible Businesses: Only restaurants and retail food establishments may operate outdoor dining as an accessory use. Annual Approvals: Regardless of location (public sidewalk or private property), all outdoor seating requires an annual administrative approval from the Planning and Zoning Department, as well as any zoning or building permits required by code. Permitted Number of Seats: Unless a specific indoor/outdoor seating allocation has been approved by the Planning and Zoning Commission, the number of seats placed outside must be deducted from the number of indoor seats on a one‑for‑one basis. ADA Accessibility: All outdoor dining areas must be fully ADA accessible, including accessible seating and clear pedestrian routes. Use of Town Sidewalks: Outdoor dining on Town‑owned sidewalks must: Maintain a minimum 6‑foot clear pedestrian path, and Provide proof of Liability Insurance if the outdoor dining area is located on a Town sidewalk. Businesses are encouraged to apply early each season to ensure timely approval. For application materials, required documentation, or guidance, please see the application form or contact the Planning and Zoning Department. ⚠ ️ Phishing Email Alert - Planning & Zoning Department The Town of Greenwich Planning and Zoning Department has identified fraudulent phishing emails impersonating Town staff. These messages are often sent from email addresses that closely resemble official Town domains and may include: Fake invoices Requests for payment References to legitimate - sounding applications 🔒 Please be aware: these are scams. Official Town email addresses always end in @greenwichct.gov or @greenwichct.org. Important Facts: We do not request payment after a decision has been made. We never send invoices by email for post - decision payments. Payments are only collected at the time of application submission. If you receive a suspicious email: Do not reply. Do not click links. Do not open attachments. Contact our office directly to confirm its legitimacy: 📞 203‑622‑7894 We apologize for any inconvenience and urge all applicants and residents to stay alert. Your cybersecurity is important to us. title: Tax Collector
address: 101 Field Point Road, 1st Floor Greenwich, CT Important: Make checks and money orders payable to Tow
hours: Home Government Departments M - Z Tax Collector Tax Collector Important Notice to North Mianus Sewer Assessment Customers: Sewer Bills were mailed out on 3/31/26. Unfortunately, due to a glitch in the service provider's system, the bills were generated at a slightly higher amount. When you pay the current billed amount, the excess funds will be applied to the payoff of next year's final bill. We apologize for any inconvenience. How to Pay Your Taxes We offer several safe and convenient ways to pay your taxes, including in person, by mail, and online . New Mobile Payment Options As of October 15, taxpayers can now pay using Apple Pay and Google Pay, making online payments faster, safer, and more convenient. You can also sign up for email and text reminders to help you stay on top of tax due dates. In - Person Payments The Tax Collector’s Office accepts checks, money orders, and cash : Monday through Friday 8:30 AM - 3:30 PM Location: Town Hall 101 Field Point Road, 1st Floor Greenwich, CT Important: Make checks and money orders payable to Town of Greenwich Please include your bill number on your check Pay by Mail Mail payments to: Tax Collector Town of Greenwich P.O. Box 3002 Greenwich, CT 06836 - 3002 Please make checks and money orders payable to Town of Greenwich and include your bill number . Pay Online Online payments are processed through our payment vendor, Point & Pay . Online Payment Options There are two ways to pay online : 1. Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT) Fee: $2.00 per transaction This convenience fee is paid directly to the vendor The fee does not go to the Town of Greenwich 2. Credit Card, Apple Pay, or Google Pay Fee: 2.50% of the total payment This convenience fee is paid directly to the vendor The fee does not go to the Town of Greenwich Important Payment Information All tax payments are recorded for interest and payment date purposes on the date they are entered into the Town’s database. A payment is not considered fully completed until the funds are successfully transferred to the Town of Greenwich’s bank account. If a payment is returned for any reason: a $20.00 returned payment fee will apply additional interest may be owed Delinquent Motor Vehicle and Personal Property Taxes The Tax Department partners with TaxServ Capital Services to collect: Delinquent Motor Vehicle Taxes Personal Property Taxes 2020 Grand List and earlier Contact TaxServ: 860 - 724 - 9100 (toll free) Delinquent Real Estate Delinquent Real Estate tax bills have been mailed out and include interest thru March 31st. Please make sure to pay no later than 3/31/26 to avoid any additional interest. Any property with a balance due will be liened in May. Motor Vehicle Taxes For motor vehicle information including Registration and Compliance issues (Property Tax, Parking Tickets, Emissions Testing, Insurance Requirements) please view our Frequently Asked Questions Numbers 9 - 16. How is the Mill Rate Determined? If you would like to know how the Mill Rate is determined, how motor vehicle tax works, what the supplemental motor vehicle tax is, what to do if you no longer own a motor vehicle that you received a tax bill for or how a Tax Sale works go to Frequently Asked Questions

Home Government Departments M - Z Town Clerk Town Clerk Announcing the Greenwich Property Alert System We are pleased to introduce our Greenwich Property Alert System. To sign up and to learn more about this new, free of charge service please visit Greenwich Property Alert Sign Up JULY 1, 2025 - NEW CONNECTICUT LAND RECORDING FEES - Effective July 1, the fee to record a Non - Nominee Land Recording will go to $70 for the 1st page, and $5 for each addition page. For Nominee land recordings, a $160 flat fee for Assignments and Releases of Mortgage where the nominee is the Grantor, and $160 for the first page and $5 for each additional page for all documents except nominee as Grantor of Assignments and Releases. Please note that new fees must be paid for any document received by the Town Clerk on or after July 1, 2025. Land Records New Land Record Public Access Search (Quick Tips & FAQs ) Our current land record search system will continue to remain active. To access please visit Greenwich CT Digital Land Records Search Please contact the town clerk's office with any questions. All Land Records are recorded daily in person in the Town Clerk's office or may be mailed via USPS, FedEx, or UPS to Town Clerk, 101 Field Point Road, Greenwich, CT 06830. The Town Clerk's office now accepts e - Recordings. Electronic Recordings - NEW to Greenwich Town Clerk Office: The Town Clerk's Office now accepts e - Recordings for select Land Record documents via 3 service providers, CSC, ePN and Simplifile. e - Recordings are accepted 8am — 2:45 pm EST Monday — Friday. Closed Holidays. CSC www.erecording.com 866 - 652 - 0111 ePN www.GOePN.com 888 - 325 - 3365 Simplifile www.simplifile.com 800 - 460 - 5657 Our land records vault is open daily during regular business hours and records are available online. Records are digitized back to the 1920s thru present day. The process of digitizing land records, from inception, is an ongoing project, as records are added the online search engine is updated. Find out how to search Digital Land Records here . Birth, Death & Marriage Certificate Copies Paperwork filled out at the hospital is the information on the certificate not a request for a certified copy. Birth Certificates are not automatically sent in Connecticut For Birth Certificate Requests CLICK HERE For Death Certificate Requests CLICK HERE For Marriage Certificate Requests CLICK HERE Purchase VITAL RECORDS ONLINE HERE Marriage Licenses Visit our marriage license page for required paperwork and further information. Appointments are required for Marriage Licenses. Dog Licenses Dogs 6 months and older need to be licensed in the State of Connecticut. Find out more here . The Greenwich Town Clerk’s Office Manages all of the Town’s Land and Vital Records Is the Secretary for the Representative Town Meeting (RTM) Is the Absentee Elections Clerk for the Town Responsibilities include: Record, index, and certify copies of all Land and Vital Records Issue Burial Permits, Dog Licenses, Marriage Licenses, Shellfish Permits Maintain records of Births, Deaths and Marriages; Town Campaign Finance and All Election Records; Claims against the Town; Justices of the Peace; Landlord and Property Registrations; Liquor Permits; Military Discharges; Notary Public Certificates; Trade Name Certificates Prepare and publish RTM Meeting agendas; compile RTM Official Minutes All Town Clerk responsibilities meet the requirements of the Town Charter, State Statutes, and Town Municipal Code. Board meetings are also videotaped and replayed on Madison Cable Channel each week. Public comment is permitted at the start of each meeting. Written comments may be sent to the Board. Calendar Mon, Apr. 27 Board of Selectmen Tue, May 12 Board of Selectmen Tue, May 26 Board of Selectmen Tue, Jun. 9 Board of Selectmen Mon, Jun. 22 Board of Selectmen View All /Calendar.aspx Overview The Madison Board of Selectmen is comprised of five (5) members consisting of a First Selectman and four (4) selectmen. Each member is elected to serve a four - year term. The First Selectman, as defined by the Town Charter (PDF), is the chief executive and chief administrative officer of the town. Following each election, the Board of Selectmen elects one selectman to serve as the Acting First Selectman to assume duties in the absence of the First Selectman. From time to time, the Board may appoint an ad - hoc committee with a specific charge and defined period of time. The Board may designate a selectman to serve on such committee. The Board designates each selectman to represent the town on various boards and commissions as ex - officio or non - voting members. If you would like to search recent real estate sales in the City and to see your property value online, follow this link https://gis.vgsi.com/norwichct/ FOR ADJUSTMENTS ON MOTOR VEHICLES THAT WERE SOLD, JUNKED, OR REGISTERED OUT OF STATE, E - MAIL PROOF TO VICTORIA VLAMOUREUX@CITYOFNORWICH.ORG OR CALL (860) 823 - 3723 CLICK HERE FOR ALLOWED PROOFS AND MOTOR VEHICLE INFORMATION Mission To ensure that all property located in the City is valued in accordance with the Connecticut General Statutes and acceptable appraisal practices. Description of Services Responsible for the discovery, listing, and valuation of all taxable and tax - exempt property in the city Ensures that all values are fair and accurate, which, in turn, guarantees that taxes are distributed in a manner that is uniform and equitable Administers myriad exemptions and abatement programs in accordance with the Connecticut General Statutes The Connecticut Office of Policy & Management provides a summary of the property tax assessment and taxation system in Connecticut . Grand List Components Real Property Real property is land and building improvements located within the city limits and is assessed at 70 percent of appraised value. Information on all properties, including assessments, may be viewed by accessing the Vision Appraisal Online Database . The last State - mandated property revaluation in the City of Norwich was performed for the October 1, 2023, Grand List. Personal Property Personal property consists of all businesses located within the city limits. In general terms, it is everything needed to engage in a business enterprise, excluding land and any improvements thereon. State statutes require all businesses to file the annual Declaration of Personal Property form on or before November 1. Failure to file a personal property declaration by November 1 would result in a 25% assessment penalty. Unregistered motor vehicles are also taxed as personal property. Vehicles Registered motorized or non - motorized vehicles (including cars, trucks, trailers, and motorcycles) are considered motor vehicles for tax purposes. The department maintains a motor vehicle list that is developed with the help of the state’s Department of Motor Vehicles. Starting with the 2024 grand list, vehicle values are based on the Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) and depreciated by a depreciation table adopted by the City of Norwich. Welcome Homeowner: On behalf of the City of Norwich, we would like to welcome you to our community. We would also like to make you aware of some programs available to you. If you feel you qualify for any of the following programs, please contact the Assessor’s Office at (860) 823 - 3723. If you wish to change your mailing address for Real Estate tax bills, click here to print the form. If you wish to change your address for a motor vehicle, please contact the Connecticut Department of Motor Vehicles . 1. Veterans: If you are a veteran of any war period so declared in the Connecticut General Statutes, you may be entitled to an exemption. You must provide an original or certified copy of your Honorable Discharge or similar document to the Norwich City Clerk. The dates of eligibility change often, so contact the Assessor’s office to make sure you qualify. Filing Deadline : September 30th. 2. Additional Veterans’ Exemption: If you receive a veteran’s exemption, you or your spouse may be eligible for an additional reduction in taxes for the next assessment year. This program has certain income requirements . Filing Period : February 1st through October 1st. 3. CT Active Duty Military and Active Duty National Guard: If you have 90 days or more cumulative service during a designated war period, you may be entitled to an exemption. You must provide a letter stating active duty status, filed annually by September 30th, with the City Clerk. If you have a vehicle, you may also be entitled to an exemption for that vehicle. Contact our office for more information regarding a vehicle exemption. 4. Leased Vehicle Exemptions: CT Active Duty Military/National Guard and Veterans may be entitled to a refund of taxes for a leased vehicle. Either the Active Duty Military Vehicle form or the Veterans Leased Vehicle form is required to be filed with the assessor. Filing Deadline: Not later than the thirty - first day of December next following the assessment year during which such tax was paid. a. CT Active Duty Military Vehicle Form b. Veterans Leased Vehicle Form 5. Disabled Veterans: Veterans claiming disability exemptions must submit proof from the Veterans Administration to the Assessor stating the percentage of disability to receive the proper amount of exemption. Upon submitting your VA disability award letter, the Assessors' office staff will determine if any other steps are necessary. Filing Deadline : You may file until the Board of Assessment Appeals completes its duties. 6. Disabled Veterans Aged 65 or Older: If you qualify for a veteran’s disability exemption and you are over 65, the exemption may be increased to the equivalent of a 100% disability rating. If you qualify, provide us with proof of age. 7. Non - Resident Active Duty Military: A non - resident active duty military service person stationed in Connecticut on military orders may be eligible for an exemption of all personal property listed in the "Service Members Civil Relief Act" including motor vehicles owned by the service member and/or spouse . Download the application form . 8. 100% Totally Disabled: Under certain circumstances, residents who are receiving permanent disability benefits under the Federal Old - Age Survivors and Disability Insurance Program or certain other qualifying programs may be entitled to receive a $1, 000 property exemption upon submission of qualifying proof to the Assessor. Download the application form . Filing Deadline : Filed until the completion of the Assessor’s Duties; Signing of the Grand List. If you qualify for this exemption, you may also qualify for the Elderly/Disabled Homeowner’s exemption. This program has certain income requi rements . Filing Period : February 1st through May 15th. 9. Blind: If you or your spouse is legally blind, you may be entitled to a $3, 000 exemption off your assessment. A Certificate of Blindness from a licensed physician or a Certificate of Blindness from the State of Connecticut Board of Education and Services for the Blind is required. Filing Deadline : File until the signing of the Grand List; January 31st. 10. Elderly or Disabled Exemption Programs: If you are a resident and the owner of the property, and you or your spouse is over 65 or considered to be permanently disabled, you may be eligible for a reduction in taxes. This program has certain income requirements Contact the Assessor’s office if you believe you may qualify. Filing Period : February 1st through May 15th. 11. Handicapped Equipped Vehicles: Under Norwich City Ordinances, local exemptions are available to owners of certain private vehicles equipped for transportation of the handicapped. Download the application form . Details are available from the Assessor. 12. Solar Exemptions: Under §12 - 81(57) of the Connecticut General Statutes, a property tax exemption is mandatory for residential property owners for any passive or active solar energy system, solar water space heating system, or geothermal energy resources. Class I renewable energy sources or an eligible hydropower facility may also be entitled to a tax exemption. A property owner must file an exemption application before November 1st in the first assessment year for which the exemption is claimed. Download the Solar and Geo - Thermal Heating Exemption form. 13. Tax Exempt Organization: Connecticut General Statutes §12 - 81(7), (10), (11), and (16) require the filing of a Tax Exempt Return seeking property tax - exempt status. Download the required documents, FAQ's, and application forms here . 14. Antique Vehicles: Vehicles registered or non - registered, 20 years or older that are defined as an antique, rare, or special interest motor vehicle, in accordance with the provisions of the Connecticut General Statutes Section 14 - 1, as amended by Public Act 08 - 150, Section 1, shall not be assessed more than $500. Filing Deadline : Owner must file Antique Vehicle Affidavit before October 1st. The City of Norwich offers many other programs to its residents. Please contact Human Services, the Recreation Department, or the Norwich Public Schools Department as appropriate for information. William M. Adopted 2025 Zoning Regulations + Zoning Map Now Available: Effective 02/15/2025 The City has conducted a comprehensive review, reformatting, and of the Zoning Regulations. The periodic update of the Zoning Regulations takes place to reflect alignment with the City's Plan of Conservation and Development (POCD), also known as Envision 06360, and current City initiatives and directives. The updated Zoning Regulations are intended to be user - friendly, use modern language, incorporate new practices and standards for development, explore opportunities for better environmental resiliency and management, and support and inspire sound economic growth throughout the City of Norwich. Should you have any questions, please reach out to Richard Shuck, Zoning Enforcement Officer at rshuck@cityofnorwich.org or Dan Daniska, City Planner / Wetland Agent at ddaniska@cityofnorwich.org . Search Mayor's State of the City Address 2026 Cultural Calendar Home Government Mayor & City Council Mayor's Dashboard Mayor's Dashboard Mayor's Message Dear Norwich Residents, First and foremost, I want to express my gratitude for the love, support, and encouragement the Norwich community gave me that encouraged me to step - up and run for this position—especially in these challenging times. I am deeply grateful to everyone who has helped me along the way. This victory is a testament to the collective consciousness of our community. It reflects what we can achieve when we come together with faith, purpose, and hope. As your mayor, my vision of Norwich is very clear: - We will strengthen our commercial tax base by bringing in new businesses and by creating an environment where businesses can grow and thrive. - We will transform our waterfront properties into spaces that generate pride, opportunity, and economic activity. - We will develop workforce housing to reshape and re - energize our downtown. - And we will restore the amenities and recreation spaces that every neighborhood deserves. Norwich must be a city where families want to live, where businesses want to invest, and where people feel connected to their community. To achieve this, we will foster a culture that is truly business - friendly and developer - friendly, while always keeping residents at the heart of every decision by increasing community participation. I aim to enhance community engagement through the mayor’s office that allows residents to play an active role in the City of Norwich. There is a lot of work ahead, and I look forward to working closely with the newly elected City Council, our City Manager and our economic partners like NPU, NCDC and Greater Norwich Area Chamber of Commerce to bring new life and new energy back to Norwich. I am also committed to partnering with our state and federal delegations to explore how to bring more resources and attention to our city including grants to revive Chestnut Street Mill and to have dedicated access to Occum Industrial Center. I always say education should be treated as part of economic development and now we will bring that to life In the Sikh faith, good governance is grounded in two essential principles: food security and social justice. These values guide me personally, and they will guide my leadership as we work to build a more equitable and stronger Norwich. Thank you again, Norwich community, for placing your trust and support in my leadership in this next chapter for the City of Norwich. I look forward to serving the City of Norwich as your Mayor. Sincerely, Mayor Swarnjit Singh Press Releases: Press Release 3 - 10 - 26 (CIF Fund Round 8) Mayor's Newsletter: Mayor Singh seeks to stay connected with the Norwich Community through his monthly "Mayor's Newsletter" which will give updates on the projects by the Mayor's Office, share upcoming Community Events, and Norwich updates. Search Home Departments Building Department / Permits Building Department / Permits The Building Department administers the permitting of residential and commercial construction within the community in conformance with the: International state - adopted codes Building Plumbing Mechanical Energy conservation Accessible and Usable Buildings and Facilities Existing Building, National Electrical codes State Statutes pertaining to Building Codes The purpose of this code is to establish the minimum requirements to safe guard the public health, safety and general welfare through structural strength, means of egress facilities, stabilities, sanitation, adequate light and ventilation, and energy conservation, and safety to life and property from fire and other hazards attributed to the built environment and to provide safety to fire fighters and emergency responders during emergency operations. Permits New building construction and/or alteration or repair of an existing building requires a building, mechanical, plumbing or electrical permit. In accordance with the State of Connecticut International Building Codes and State adopted Amendments. A building permit is required when the intent is to construct, enlarge, alter, repair, move, demolish, or change the occupancy of a building or structure. Or to erect, install, enlarge, alter, repair, remove, convert or replace any electrical, gas, mechanical, or plumbing system, the installation of which is regulated by this code, or cause any work to be done, shall first make application to the building Official and obtain the required permits. Time Limitation of Application An application for a permit for any proposed work shall be deemed to have been abandoned 180 days after the date of filing, unless such application has been pursued in good faith. The applicant may in writing request an extension and justifiable cause demonstrated. Search Home Departments Tax Collector Tax Collector Due Dates Tax bills are mailed once a year: First installment bills are due no later than August 1, 2025 Second installment bills are due no later than February 2, 2026 Please review our FAQs for additional information about due dates and penalties. Mill Rate The 2024 - 2025 mill rate for real estate, personal property, and motor vehicles 29.17. (pertains to bills due July 1, 2024 and January 1, 2025). The 2025 - 2026 mill rate for real estate, personal property, and motor vehicles 30.24. (pertains to bills due July 1, 2025 and January 1, 2026). Online Bill Payment Online Bill Payment offers the following benefits: Access, view and pay your bills online, at your convenience Paying your bill online is faster and easier than writing a check and mailing it You can pay with electronic check or credit/debit card Payments will not be reflected online for 48 to 72 business hours after payment is made Convenience Fee Use of a credit card will incur a convenience fee of 2.50% with a minimum charge of $2. The use of a debit card will incur a fee of $3.95. The Town of Rocky Hill receives only your bill payment amount. Convenience fees cover various administrative costs associated with accepting payments and are non - refundable. The convenience fee amount is automatically calculated and is shown on the payment page before you submit your payment for processing. Convenience fees will appear as a separate charge from the bill amount on your bank or credit card statement. Use of an electronic check (e - check) will not incur a convenience fee. Search Home Government Office of the Mayor Office of the Mayor Allan Smith Elected in November 2025, Mayor Smith is focused on keeping Rocky Hill affordable, accountable, and open for business. His leadership emphasizes steady economic growth, responsible budgeting, and transparent local government. Mayor Smith believes in building a community where residents and businesses can thrive. He is committed to revitalizing targeted areas, supporting small businesses, improving walkability, and investing in the town’s public spaces, arts, and sustainability. Dedicated to both preserving Rocky Hill’s historic character and planning for its future, Mayor Smith works to ensure that growth benefits all residents and strengthens the community. Tell Us Who Rocks Help us spotlight excellence. Tell us who rocks! Every quarter, we will recognize 10 people who go above and beyond in providing excellent service to the community. Nominees must be a Rocky Hill resident, or local business, or Town employee. Download and submit the You Rock Nomination Form (PDF) today!
